negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Missing scheduler event decoder overflow rejection. The old scheduling event decoder is absent locally; storage-reading code converts U256 storage fields with to::<u64>() but does not implement event decoding rejection for oversized event identifiers.
related_but_insufficient_code: No u256_to_u64/EventDecodingError/EnhancedCallEvent symbols were found in the audited local repos.

rationale: The current event parser routes U256 scheduler event identifiers and execution counters through u256_to_u64, which returns SchedulingError::EventDecodingError on overflow, before storing them in u64 fields.
statement: Scheduler event decoding must reject oversized U256 values for callId and for execution counters such as executionIndex and totalExecutions when those values exceed u64::MAX, rather than silently truncating them into u64 state.
suggested_falsification_tests: Decode Scheduler events with callId, executionIndex, and totalExecutions set to u64::MAX + 1 and assert EventDecodingError.
Assert no truncating u64 state is produced from oversized U256 event fields.
summary: Scheduler event overflow rejection is absent.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Current port lacks crates/scheduling/src/contract.rs and the u256_to_u64 event-overflow rejection path.
title: Scheduler event decoding rejects oversized identifiers
